title: @as(d64, binary_float_expr) silently produces inexact decimal — no compile detection
description: The spec warns 'Do not use @as(d64, 0.1)' but the compiler allows it. @as(d64, x) where x is a binary float produces an inexact decimal. The type system cannot distinguish 'decimal literal that is exact' from 'binary float cast to decimal'. A caller initializing d64 values from f64 constants or computations silently gets wrong results. What caller observes: financial calculations off by small non-obvious amounts. The correctness invariant is enforced by documentation, not by the type.
